decks.notes: This deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana quickly and efficiently through cards like Mana Vault, Moxen, and mana rocks, then using Paradoxical Outcome to draw a ton of cards and replaying artifacts for more mana and synergy. The goal is to assemble a game-winning combo or overwhelming advantage in resources to secure victory. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value and recursion for smaller utility creatures or artifacts. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Chain of Vapor to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the necessary pieces to cast Doomsday, set up a winning pile of cards with Thassa's Oracle, and win the game on the same turn. The deck includes powerful card draw and filtering effects like Ancestral Recall, Ponder, and Dig Through Time to find key combo pieces while disrupting opponent's plans with counterspells like Force of Will and Flusterstorm. It also includes tutors like Demonic Tutor and Vampiric Tutor to search for important combo pieces and protection spells. The deck aims to win the game quickly and efficiently through its combo strategy.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with artifacts like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith to play powerful threats such as Glaring Fleshraker and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void to slow down opponents while generating card advantage with cards like Sensei's Divining Top and Mystic Forge. The inclusion of cards like Time Vault and The One Ring offer alternate win conditions while cards like Strip Mine and Wasteland disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive threats and powerful artifacts.

This deck focuses on using control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while generating card advantage and setting up powerful win conditions. It combines counterspells like Force of Will and Force of Negation with card draw spells like Brainstorm and Ancestral Recall to maintain control of the game. The deck also includes powerful artifacts like Time Vault and Bolas's Citadel, which can provide alternate win conditions. Tezzeret, Cruel Captain can be used to tutor for key artifacts, while Karn, the Great Creator can shut down opponents' artifacts. Overall, the deck aims to dominate the game through control and powerful card advantage.

The primary focus of this deck is to maintain control over the game by disrupting the opponent's strategy while assembling powerful combos to quickly close out the game. By utilizing a combination of counterspells, card advantage, and powerful artifacts like Time Vault and Bolas's Citadel, the deck aims to dominate the battlefield and dictate the pace of the match. With a mix of efficient removal spells and powerful win conditions, such as Tinker and Yawgmoth's Will, the Dimir Control deck is designed to outmaneuver and outclass the opponent in a Vintage 1 vs 1 match.

This deck focuses on playing aggressive artifact creatures and disrupting the opponent's game plan through cards like Null Rod, Sphere of Resistance, and Thorn of Amethyst. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient threats like Stonecoil Serpent and Golos, Tireless Pilgrim, while also using powerful artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator and Mystic Forge to generate card advantage and finish the game quickly. The deck utilizes powerful mana acceleration from Mishra's Workshop and the Moxen to cast multiple spells in a turn and control the board with cards like Crucible of Worlds and Wasteland.

The main concept of this Vintage 1 vs 1 Aggro - Hatebear deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy by employing hate cards like Void Mirror and Chancellor of the Annex, while also putting pressure on them with efficient creatures like Archon of Emeria and White Plume Adventurer. The deck utilizes powerful artifacts like Black Lotus and Chrome Mox for fast mana acceleration, and also has disruptive lands like Wasteland and Karakas. Overall, the deck aims to establish a disruptive board presence and control the game tempo to secure victory.

This deck focuses on using the powerful card draw and bounce effects of Paradoxical Outcome to generate a large amount of mana and card advantage, allowing for explosive plays and ultimately winning the game through a combo finish. The deck includes key cards such as Time Vault, Tezzeret, and Tinker to assemble the combo pieces needed for victory. It also includes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Narset to protect the combo and interact with opposing strategies. This deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and shut down opposing strategies for a decisive win.

The deck focuses on using powerful artifacts and spells to assemble a winning combination that revolves around the use of Coveted Jewel. By accelerating mana with cards like Mishra's Workshop, Tolarian Academy, and Grim Monolith, the deck aims to quickly play and activate Coveted Jewel to draw cards and gain a significant advantage over the opponent. Cards like Transmute Artifact and Karn, the Great Creator provide additional ways to tutor for key pieces of the combo, while disruption and protection are provided by cards like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing counterspells and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining card advantage through cards like Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion allows for additional value by recurring low-cost permanents. The deck also includes efficient mana acceleration with cards like Black Lotus and the Moxen, enabling the player to cast their spells quickly and efficiently. With a mix of card draw, disruption, and threats, Esper Control aims to outplay and outlast the opponent in a game of resource management.

This deck relies on disrupting opponent's strategies while pushing forward with aggressive creatures. The Hatebear creatures like Archon of Emeria and Anointed Peacekeeper limit your opponent's options, while Seasoned Dungeoneer and White Plume Adventurer provide card advantage. The deck also incorporates disruptive artifacts like Void Mirror and Chalice of the Void, along with hate cards like Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator. With fast mana sources like Chrome Mox and Ancient Tomb, this deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver opponents in the Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on using disruptive cards like Force of Will, Swords to Plowshares, and Lavinia, Azorius Renegade to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It also utilizes powerful spells like Ancestral Recall, Time Walk, and Dig Through Time to gain card advantage and generate value. The inclusion of a companion in Lurrus of the Dream-Den allows for additional recursion and flexibility in gameplay. The mana base is streamlined with fetch lands and powerful mana rocks like Black Lotus, Mox Pearl, Mox Jet, and Mox Sapphire to ensure consistent access to the necessary colors of mana.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 deck focuses on using aggressive MUD artifacts to disrupt opponents' strategies and control the board. Mishra's Workshop and other mana acceleration cards help quickly deploy threats like Patchwork Automaton and Golos, Tireless Pilgrim. Sphere of Resistance and Thorn of Amethyst slow down opponents, while Null Rod disrupts artifact-heavy strategies. Crucible of Worlds and Wasteland help control opponents' lands, while Karn, the Great Creator can tutor for key artifacts. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with efficient threats and disrupt their game plan with its disruptive elements.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and aggressively play powerful artifacts to overwhelm the opponent. Cards like Grim Monolith, Lodestone Golem, and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain help to ramp up mana production and put pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Black Lotus, Time Vault, and Mana Crypt allows for explosive plays and game-changing turns. The deck also has utility cards like Wasteland and Pithing Needle to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to establish board control early and maintain a strong presence to secure victory.

The primary focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful artifacts, while also disrupting their strategy with control elements. The deck utilizes fast mana sources like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith to accelerate its game plan, along with key cards like Trinisphere and Chalice of the Void to hinder the opponent's actions. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like The One Ring and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain helps provide additional threats and card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to quickly establish dominance on the battlefield and maintain control throughout the game.

The deck focuses on controlling the game and disrupting the opponent's strategy while setting up the combo of Oath of Druids to cheat powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play. It also uses cards like Oko, Thief of Crowns and Show and Tell to further manipulate the game to its advantage. The deck includes a mix of powerful spells and mana acceleration to help establish board presence and maintain control throughout the game. Additionally, it has a few versatile sideboard options to adapt to different matchups and strategies.
